response.justification: While Bogdan brings exceptional software engineering depth with 20 years of experience across full-stack, mobile, and infrastructure domains, this role requires demonstrated people management and formal technical leadership—critical gaps in his resume. He shows no evidence of managing direct reports, conducting performance reviews, or developing teams—core responsibilities for a Level II Manager position. His background as an individual contributor, open-source founder, and technical mentor, while impressive, does not prepare him for the people-leadership competencies essential to this engineering productivity management role.
strengths: Extensive 20-year software development background across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems, significantly exceeding the 8-year minimum requirement

Demonstrated technical depth with multiple Staff-level roles and hands-on exposure to developer infrastructure (GitLab, CI/CD, Docker, Artifactory, MCP frameworks) relevant to engineering productivity
Strong mentorship and communication capabilities evidenced through organizing Refactoring Dojos, leading workshops, teaching courses, and contributing to open-source projects
concerns: No evidence of people management experience—no mention of managing direct reports, conducting performance reviews, or team development, which is a required minimum 2-year requirement for this management role

Limited formal technical leadership demonstrated; while holding Staff titles, the resume lacks explicit examples of setting team technical direction, validating system designs for teams, or leading organizational change

Developer tools and build systems are not a primary career focus; experience is scattered across mobile apps, data platforms, and full-stack work rather than concentrated in engineering productivity infrastructure
